purpose: fixed bounded-continuation failure set for multi-input continuation and alias-result stitching work

selection_basis: targets current execution-contract gaps rather than already-green two-hop scope extensions
keeps the cases small enough to become fixtures/regressions in later G12 tasks
separates input-binding failures from alias-family and provenance failures
excluded_current_controls: ruby two-hop require_relative wrapper return continuation
basic imported-result into caller alias chain

defaults.note: engine-difference evaluation stays out of this set; this set isolates bounded continuation execution gaps

cases.focus: relevant second argument should survive a nested multi-input continuation through wrap -> pair

initial_source.pair.rs: pub fn pair(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 { b + 1 }

present: pair.rs side relevant input is still visible
wrapper-local temp result can still be seen

cases.why_it_matters: this is the smallest current frontier where continuation must stay selective across a nested multi-input summary instead of relying on a single-input fallback

cases.focus: reordered and partial input binding should still carry the relevant caller input into the nested summary and back to the caller result

initial_source.pair.rs: pub fn pair(left: i32, _lit: i32, right: i32) -> i32 { left + 1 }

absent_or_weak: binding y to pair(left) after reordering
ignoring the literal slot as a partial binding

cases.why_it_matters: G12 needs an input-binding test that breaks zip-by-order semantics without introducing unrelated scope complications

cases.focus: imported result should flow through wrapper-local alias and caller-local alias before closing at out

initial_source.value.rs: pub fn make(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }

absent_or_weak: stable mixed stitching across tmp -> alias -> y -> out
family-level explanation that this was alias-result stitching rather than a generic bridge

cases.why_it_matters: this is the smallest case where alias-result stitching must survive both a wrapper alias zone and a caller alias zone

cases.focus: a tier2 boundary_alias_continuation candidate should be able to continue one more hop into the same alias family

initial_source.shared.rs: pub fn source(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }

present: value.rs can plausibly win as a tier2 boundary_alias_continuation candidate
absent_or_weak: tier3 continuation metadata that preserves boundary_alias_continuation

cases.why_it_matters: current tier3 continuation is still wrapper-return-biased, so G12 needs a case that measures alias-family continuation rather than plain return-family continuation

cases.focus: require_relative split and alias-result stitching should close as one mixed continuation chain

initial_source.main.rb:
require_relative "lib/wrap"

def entry
  x = 1
  y = Wrap.wrap(x)
  out = y
  puts out
end
initial_source.lib/wrap.rb:
require_relative "value"

module Wrap
  def self.wrap(a)
    tmp = Value.make(a)
    alias_value = tmp
    alias_value
  end
end
initial_source.lib/value.rb:
require_relative "leaf"

module Value
  def self.make(a)
    mid = Leaf.leaf(a)
    mid
  end
end
initial_source.lib/leaf.rb:
module Leaf
  def self.leaf(a)
    a + 1
  end
end

absent_or_weak: a single mixed chain from leaf through alias_value to out
compact provenance that shows both require_relative and alias-result stitching

cases.why_it_matters: G12 should not become Rust-only; Ruby needs at least one mixed continuation case where require_relative provenance and alias-result stitching must cooperate
